text: Specify concatenated child-phase windowing and artificial scrolling behavior
description: Specify and implement column scrolling as a virtual list over three element types: phase items, empty placeholders, and non-selectable parent-boundary separators. The selected-path item should be kept roughly centered, with overscan/overflow covering at least 50% of viewport height before and after the visible region. j/k should first consume partial scrolling within the current level when the next target edge is offscreen, then advance selection. Moving in a sublevel updates parent selections recursively when parent ownership changes, while the focused level alone remains active. If a parent has no children, include a selectable placeholder row that supports creating the first child via o/O; because there are no siblings yet, o and O are identical in that empty case. Remember child position by index per parent when possible, and if the child set changed while away, remembered child index should win when still valid and otherwise fall back to the nearest valid in-bounds index.

status.comment: Finalized windowing, placeholder, and cross-parent navigation behavior for implementation.
